description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service and defines the variables CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE, CONTAINER_RELEASE_IMAGE, REPOSITORY_URL, AWS_PUBLIC_IP, AWS_SSH, AWS_USER and DOCKER_DRIVER. Add a build job in the build stage that runs the global before_script to log in to the GitLab registry, then executes docker build --pull -t $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE . and docker push $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE, and runs only on the develop and master branches. Add three test jobs in the test stage—test_behat runs docker run -d --name ci $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE followed by echo "behat"; test_cs runs echo "phpcs"; and test_unit runs docker run -d --name ci $CONTAINER_TEST_IMAGE and docker exec ci composer test—each limited to develop and master. Add a deploy job in the deploy stage that installs curl, jq, python and pip via apk, installs awscli with pip, logs into AWS ECR using $(aws ecr get-login --no-include-email --region eu-west-1), pulls the test image, tags it with $REPOSITORY_URL and pushes it to the ECR repository, and runs only on the master branch. Finally, add a purge job in the purge stage that simply echoes "Purge not configured" and also runs only on master.
